rationale: Ground truth has no substantive reviewer concerns to recall, so both are roughly equal there. Both correctly validate the core `==10` fix and independently raise the same sm_110a edge-case concern (plausible, likely sourced from the PR description). Y goes further with two additional grounded, actionable nits (missing sm_101 test case, doc heading/body inconsistency) with precise line references, while X's single suggestion ('`in (10,)` phrased so 11.x can be added later') is logically muddled since that syntax doesn't actually add 11.x support. Y's greater specificity and correctness in its actionable suggestions gives it a slight edge.
